author: Christopher Mayer
source: 100 Baggers: Stocks That Return 100-to-1 and How To Find Them (2015)
description: Quantitative gate and qualitative overlay for identifying extreme compounders capable of returning 100x over 15-25 years. Pre-computed metrics are passed as facts; Gemini writes narrative rationale only.

quantitative_gate.description: Hard filters evaluated in Python before any LLM call. Gemini receives pass/fail per rule and the raw metric — it does NOT re-evaluate thresholds.

rules.name: Acorn Principle — Market Cap
rules.description: Companies must be small enough to multiply 100-fold. Large companies cannot achieve this geometrically.
rules.note: Most portfolio holdings will fail this gate — that is expected and informative.

rules.name: Compounding Efficiency — ROIC
rules.description: Return on Invested Capital measures how efficiently the business converts capital into earnings. Sustained high ROIC is mathematical proof of a durable moat.
rules.note: Use key-metrics TTM endpoint. If ROIC unavailable, use ROE as a proxy.

rules.name: Top-Line Revenue Growth
rules.description: High ROIC without growth produces no compounding. Organic revenue growth is required.
rules.note: Computed as 3-year CAGR from income statement. Prefer 3yr over 1yr to smooth volatility.

rules.name: Gross Margin — Moat Indicator
rules.description: Structural gross margins above 50% are mathematical proof of pricing power and competitive advantage competitors cannot undercut.
rules.note: For capital-intensive sectors (energy, mining, industrials), apply the soft threshold.

rules.name: Dividend Leak — Capital Allocation
rules.description: Dividends are a leak. A 100-bagger candidate must reinvest all free cash flow internally or via buybacks.
rules.note: A 0% payout is ideal. Penalize, do not hard-reject, on this rule alone.

quantitative_gate.overall_pass_logic: MUST pass acorn, roic, and revenue_growth. gross_margin soft pass acceptable for capital-intensive sectors. dividend_payout is a soft penalty, not a hard reject.

qualitative_overlay.description: Evaluated by Gemini from thesis file and market context — only if quantitative gate passed or is borderline.

pillars.name: Skin in the Game
pillars.description: Insider ownership ideally > 10%. Founder-led businesses with founder still active are heavily favored.
signals: Founder still active in CEO / Executive Chair role
Low executive base salary relative to equity comp

pillars.name: The Twin Engines
pillars.description: 100-bagger requires BOTH earnings growth AND multiple expansion. Evaluate current valuation relative to growth rate.
signals: Current P/E or EV/EBITDA is reasonable relative to growth rate (PEG < 1.5)
Expanding gross margins signal operating leverage ahead
Market does not yet fully recognize the compounding potential

pillars.name: Coffee-Can Holding Mindset
pillars.description: Would you be comfortable putting this in a coffee can and forgetting about it for 10+ years?
signals: Not dependent on a single customer or contract
Management communicates clearly with shareholders (Baid candor test)

recommendation_levels.STRONG_BUY: Passes all quantitative gates. Strong qualitative overlay. Conviction compounder — coffee-can candidate.
recommendation_levels.WATCHLIST: Passes most quantitative gates but fails 1-2 (often market cap, which will shrink as position matures). Worth monitoring for entry timing.
recommendation_levels.REJECT: Fails core quantitative gate (ROIC < 15%, revenue growth < 10%, or market cap > $5B). Cannot achieve 100x from current base.

scoring_guidance.note: Gemini writes narrative for each evaluation field. Python provides the raw metric and pass/fail — Gemini explains WHY it matters in context.
scoring_guidance.acorn_evaluation: State whether market cap passes the gate. For large-cap holdings (NVDA, GOOG etc.), explain why they cannot achieve 100x but may still be excellent GARP holds.
scoring_guidance.roic_evaluation: Compare ROIC to the 18% threshold. If using ROE as proxy, note it.
scoring_guidance.growth_evaluation: Comment on revenue growth trajectory — accelerating, decelerating, or stable.
scoring_guidance.margin_evaluation: Comment on gross margin level and trend — is the moat widening or compressing?
scoring_guidance.dividend_evaluation: For 0% payout: reward. For high payout: flag as capital allocation concern.
scoring_guidance.fundamental_reason: One sentence summary: 'NVDA has grown beyond the Acorn threshold but remains a GARP compounder with unmatched ROIC.'
